eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 5 年级

chrome.sub: Someone has been following a student across the internet — quietly, from site to site. Read the clues, solve four locks, and expose how the tracking works.
chrome.briefP: Websites can leave tiny files called cookies that follow you as you move from page to page. Your job is to learn how a digital footprint forms and how data trails you online. Crack all four locks, and each one will show the reason its answer is correct.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You now know what builds a digital footprint, how to match your writing to your audience, what happens when you copy work without permission, and how to spot a secured connection. Sharp detective work!

clues.title: How does data follow you?
clues.body: As you visit websites, some of them save small files called cookies on your device. These cookies can remember you and follow your navigation from one site to the next. That is how trackers learn where you have been.

clues.title: What builds a footprint?
clues.body: A digital footprint is made from what you do online. The games you play, the videos you watch, and the posts you share all become part of it. Things you do offline, like reading a paper book, do not.

clues.title: Who are you writing to?
clues.body: Good online etiquette means matching your words to your audience. A message to your teacher should be polite and use full, careful sentences. A quick note to a close friend can be more casual and relaxed.

clues.title: Using someone's creation
clues.body: If you use another person's photo, song, or writing without permission or credit, there are real consequences. The creator can ask you to take it down, and you could get in trouble. The honest path is to ask first and give credit.

clues.title: Is this connection safe?
clues.body: A secured website shows a small padlock symbol near its address, and the address starts with the letters h-t-t-p-s. The 's' stands for secure. Count the letters in 'https' and remember that number — it proves the connection is protected.

clues.title: The reading room pet
clues.body: The school library has a pet hamster named Pixel who lives near the computers. Pixel naps all morning and runs on his wheel at night. This is a fun fact, but it will not help you open any lock.

locks.title: Parts of the Footprint
locks.q: Check EVERY thing that becomes part of a digital footprint.

items.t: A video you watched on a streaming app
A level you beat on an online game
A photo you posted on a social page
A paper book you read quietly at home
A walk you took outside with no phone

locks.reason: A digital footprint is built from what you do online — watching, playing, and posting all leave a trail, while reading a paper book or walking outside happens offline.

locks.title: Match Your Audience
locks.q: You are writing a message to your TEACHER. Which one fits the audience best?
options: yo!!! did we hav hw?? lol just tell me quick
Hello Ms. Lopez, could you please tell me if we have homework tonight? Thank you.
u forgot to say the homework fix that pls
locks.reason: Good etiquette means matching your words to your reader, and a message to a teacher should be polite and written in full, careful sentences.

locks.title: The Secure Code
locks.q: A safe website's address starts with 'https'. How many letters are in the word 'https'?
locks.reason: The word 'https' has five letters, and that 's' at the end stands for secure — the sign of a protected connection.

locks.title: The Fair Way to Borrow
locks.q: You want to use someone else's photo. Tap the steps in the RIGHT order.
locks.reason: First ASK the creator for permission, then plan to give them CREDIT, and only then USE the work — that avoids the consequences of copying without permission.
